findings: HUMAN_APPROVAL_REQUIRED lifecycle is replay-visible and preserves human authority.
Approval resume validates chain, scope, proposal, approval request hash, expiration, and replay packet integrity.
Interactive CLI currently resumes only on a literal approve turn.
Reject and modification request paths are not first-class implementation approval outcomes.
show-latest-chain and related inspection commands are source-replay read-only but write inspection report evidence.
Replay inspection fail-closes safely but needs clearer operator diagnostics.
Unknown domain handling is deterministic but does not provide supported registry feedback to the operator.
blocking_gaps: Missing implementation approval modification request path.
Replay inspection failure messages are too coarse for OCS operator use.
Registry resolution feedback does not list supported domains or executable status on failure.
recommended_fix_order: First-class rejection artifact and terminal replay outcome.
First-class modification request artifact and bounded continuation.
Registry resolution feedback for unknown or unsupported domains.

validation_commands.command: Review-only source inspection with rg and sed.
validation_commands.summary: Approval lifecycle, resume runtime, chain inspection, replay reconstruction, registry, routing, and tests were inspected.
